scope: Forward-carried compressed-group sidecar consumed inside the fast query-major sparse-MLA dKV backward producer

mode15.name: forward_sidecar_query_major_sink
mode15.read: Loads group_ids[B,S,K] for the selected window once into shared memory, keeps the K16 query-major score/prob/scaled-dP loop, keeps the H16 wave-partial dKV reduction, and replaces final compressed-dKV atomics with a checksum sink.

mode16.name: forward_sidecar_query_major_valid_dkv
mode16.read: Same producer and sidecar contract as mode15, but emits final compressed-dKV global atomics.

decision.why: This is the requested sidecar-in-fast-loop contract: q remains the BF16 numeric activation, group_ids[B,S,K] is carried from forward and loaded beside the normal query-major producer, and the group ids are used only in the dKV epilogue. It avoids the rejected full K512 prob/scaled-dP page and avoids the rejected token-tile group-panel owner. The measured latency is still 22.96 ms for valid dKV, about 2.06x slower than the 11.13 ms sparse-attention backward boundary gate. The checksum split shows final dKV atomics add only about 1.48 ms, so the remaining wall is producer plus H-wave contribution reduction/epilogue work inside the one-token CTA, not the final global atomic alone.
decision.next_kernel_contract: Keep this forward sidecar contract, but do not keep trying old mode knobs. The next real kernel needs the same one-token query-major producer plus a lower-level epilogue that reduces selected contributions by compressed group before per-contribution H16 fragments hit shared/global memory, or a cooperative persistent producer/reducer that preserves q/dO reuse while creating cross-token group ownership.
